sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: This Round 33 La Liga clash sees Atlético Madrid, sitting in 3rd place with 67 points, host Rayo Vallecano, who are in 8th place with 44 points. The match takes place at the Riyadh Air Metropolitano in Madrid, a fortress for the home side. With Atlético Madrid boasting a strong league position, they are clear favorites against a mid-table Rayo Vallecano side.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: In the last 5 matchups between these teams, Atlético Madrid has dominated with 3 wins and 2 draws, leaving Rayo Vallecano without a victory. Their most recent encounter on September 22, 2024, ended in a 1-1 draw at Rayo’s ground, with Atlético’s Nahuel Molina earning Player of the Match honors with a 7.6 Sofascore rating. Historically, Atlético Madrid has an incredible record against Rayo, remaining unbeaten in their last 17 head-to-head games, often being the first to score in 9 of their last 10 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Atlético Madrid comes into this match with a concerning streak of 6 games without a clean sheet, though this run has recently ended. Rayo Vallecano, on the other hand, is struggling with a 3-game winless streak and has failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 3 matches. Head-to-head trends suggest both teams scoring in 4 of their last 5 encounters, though this streak is not currently active, while corners are often kept under 10.5 in 6 of their last 8 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Atlético Madrid Key Players
sections.text: Atlético Madrid’s attack is led by Julián Álvarez, who has scored 15 goals against an expected goals (xG) of 12.41, overperforming by 2.5864. Alexander Sørloth adds firepower with 13 goals, while Antoine Griezmann contributes with 8 goals and 6 assists, boasting a solid 7.21 average rating. In defense, Axel Witsel stands out with a 73.9% ground duels won rate, ranking 2nd in the league, making him a crucial asset. Midfielders like Marcos Llorente, with a 7.19 rating, and Rodrigo de Paul also play vital roles in maintaining balance.

sections.subtitle: Rayo Vallecano Key Players
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano’s offense relies on Álvaro García, who has 3 goals and 5 assists despite underperforming his xG by 3.2817, yet holds a strong 7.03 average rating. Isi Palazón adds creativity with 3 goals and 4 assists, while Pathé Ismaël Ciss has overperformed his xG by 2.1167, scoring 4 goals. Defensively, Florian Lejeune stands out with a 7.04 rating and 2 assists, providing stability at the back for Rayo.

sections.subtitle: Standout Historical Performer
sections.text: In the last 5 matches against Rayo Vallecano, Atlético’s Javi Galán has been a standout performer with an average Sofascore rating of 7.58 over 4 starts. Although he hasn’t scored, he provided 1 assist, showing his impact in these fixtures. His consistency could be a factor if he features in this matchup at the Riyadh Air Metropolitano.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics and Style
sections.text: Atlético Madrid has scored 56 goals this season, ranking 4th in the league, while conceding only 27, the 2nd least in La Liga, with 15 clean sheets to their name, the best in the competition. They convert 44.21% of big chances, ranking 3rd, and have created 95 big chances, also 4th in the league. Rayo Vallecano, while less dominant, shows resilience, though they struggle to keep clean sheets, especially away, as seen in their last 14 head-to-head games against Atlético.

sections.subtitle: Referee Impact
sections.text: The match will be officiated by Pablo González Fuertes, who averages 5.5 yellow cards per game and 0.34 red cards per game. Given Atlético Madrid’s tendency for less than 4.5 cards in 5 of their last 7 home games and their league average of 2.12 yellow cards per game, we can expect a potentially heated encounter. Rayo Vallecano’s disciplinary record may also be tested under this referee’s strict whistle.

sections.subtitle: Goal Timing Trends
sections.text: Atlético Madrid tends to strike late, with 25.93% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes, totaling 20 goals in this period. Rayo Vallecano also scores frequently late in games, with 26.19% of their goals between the 76th and 90th minutes, amounting to 8 goals. Both teams could push for decisive strikes in the closing stages of this match.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players
sections.text: Atlético Madrid will be without midfielder Pablo Barrios, who has contributed 1 goal and 3 assists this season with a 6.91 average rating. Rayo Vallecano misses forward Sergio Camello, with 3 goals and 2 assists, and defender Abdul Mumin, who has 2 goals and a 6.89 rating. These absences could impact both teams’ depth, particularly Rayo’s attacking options.
